content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Updated the milestone filesystem commit path in `src/adapters/fs.rs` to reuse the already-loaded pre-mutation journal when building `PendingMilestoneStateCommit` instead of rereading `journal.ndjson`, and documented the intentional cleanup asymmetry around pending-sidecar removal: snapshot-write failure discards the sidecar because `write_atomic` leaves the old state intact, while journal-write failure preserves it so locked recovery can finish the atomic publish.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspected `commit_snapshot_and_journal_ops` in the filesystem milestone journal adapter to confirm the redundant journal reread and the undocumented pending-sidecar failure semantics.
2. [Completed] Patched the adapter to clone the pre-mutation journal for `previous_journal` and added comments explaining why snapshot failure removes the pending file while journal failure intentionally leaves it behind for recovery.
3. [Completed] Ran the filesystem unit-test slice covering milestone pending-state commit behavior to verify the amended crash-recovery path still passes.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo test fs:: --lib` passed (17 tests).

## Outstanding Risks

- `MilestoneJournalPort::commit_snapshot_and_journal_ops()` still has a sequential default implementation for non-filesystem ports; any future production port must override it to preserve the stronger filesystem behavior.
